    83|export type IVXGatePipelineInput = {

   111|
   112|export type IVXGatePipelineStageResult = {
   113|  gate: 'pre_execution_feasibility' | 'fake_execution' | 'senior_developer_narrative' | 'access_status_narrative' | 'reliability';
   114|  gated: boolean;
   115|  state: IVXFakeExecutionState | IVXIAState;

   119|
   120|export type IVXGatePipelineResult = {
   121|  /** The final gated answer to return to the caller. */
   122|  answer: string;
   123|  /** True when any gate intervened and rewrote the answer. */
   124|  gated: boolean;
   125|  /** The single resolved decision state for this reply. */
   126|  state: IVXIAState;
   127|  /** Per-stage audit trail (in execution order). Never empty. */
   128|  stages: IVXGatePipelineStageResult[];
   129|  /** The final reason from the last gate that intervened, or null when no
   130|   *  gate intervened. */
   131|  reason: string | null;
   132|};

   134|/**
   135| * Run the unified IVX IA gate pipeline. Deterministic, pure, no I/O.
   136| *
   137| * Each gate receives the previous gate's output answer so the pipeline
   138| * composes. The first gate that intervenes records its state; subsequent
   139| * gates still run so the final `state` reflects the most restrictive
   140| * resolution (the reliability gate runs last and has the final word on the
   141| * single-state header).
   142| */
   143|/**
   144| * Inline (synchronous) feasibility check used when the caller did not pre-run
   145| * the gate. Presence-only — no live network probes — so it is safe to run in
   146| * the pure pipeline. The full async gate (with live probes) is run by the
   147| * execution loop and passed in via `input.feasibility`.
   148| */
   149|function runInlineFeasibilityCheck(message: string, ownerSessionPresent: boolean): FeasibilityGateResult {
   150|  const intent = classifyTaskIntent(message);
   151|  const required = requiredCapabilitiesFor(intent);
   152|  const generatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
   153|  if (required.length === 0) {
   154|    return {
   155|      state: 'READY',
   156|      taskId: 'inline-feasibility',
   157|      capabilities: [],
   158|      repeatedBlocker: false,
   159|      generatedAt,
   160|      marker: 'ivx-pre-execution-feasibility-gate-2026-07-05-v1' as const,
   161|    };
   162|  }
   163|  // Inline check: only verify owner session (the only capability that does not
   164|  // need a live probe). Credential checks defer to the async gate.
   165|  if (required.includes('verify_owner_session') && !ownerSessionPresent) {
   166|    return {
   167|      state: 'BLOCKED',
   168|      taskId: 'inline-feasibility',
   169|      blockerCode: 'OWNER_SESSION_MISSING',
   170|      exactBlocker: 'No verified owner session is present. Owner login is required before this task can execute.',
   171|      failedCapability: 'verify_owner_session',
   172|      requiredVariable: 'IVX_OWNER_TOKEN',
   173|      runtimeSource: 'none',
   174|      httpStatus: null,
   175|      nextOwnerAction: 'Complete owner login. A verified owner session is required before this task can execute.',
   176|      capabilities: [],
   177|      repeatedBlocker: false,
   178|      generatedAt,
   179|      marker: 'ivx-pre-execution-feasibility-gate-2026-07-05-v1' as const,
   180|    };
   181|  }
   182|  return {
   183|    state: 'READY',
   184|    taskId: 'inline-feasibility',
   185|    capabilities: [],
   186|    repeatedBlocker: false,
   187|    generatedAt,
   188|    marker: 'ivx-pre-execution-feasibility-gate-2026-07-05-v1' as const,
   189|  };
   190|}
   191|
   192|/** Format the inline feasibility block for the chat answer. */
   193|function formatInlineFeasibilityBlock(result: FeasibilityGateResult): string {
   194|  if (result.state === 'READY') return '';
   195|  return [
   196|    'STATE: BLOCKED',
   197|    `TASK_ID: ${result.taskId}`,
   198|    `BLOCKER_CODE: ${result.blockerCode}`,
   199|    `EXACT_BLOCKER: ${result.exactBlocker}`,
   200|    `FAILED_CAPABILITY: ${result.failedCapability}`,
   201|    `REQUIRED_VARIABLE: ${result.requiredVariable ?? 'n/a'}`,
   202|    `RUNTIME_SOURCE: ${result.runtimeSource}`,
   203|    `HTTP_STATUS: ${result.httpStatus ?? 'n/a'}`,
   204|    `NEXT_OWNER_ACTION: ${result.nextOwnerAction}`,
   205|    'Pre-execution feasibility gate blocked execution. The chat model cannot fabricate this task.',
   206|  ].join('\n');
   207|}
   208|
   209|export function runIVXUnifiedGatePipeline(input: IVXGatePipelineInput): IVXGatePipelineResult {
   210|  const stages: IVXGatePipelineStageResult[] = [];
   211|  let answer = input.answer;
   212|  let anyGated = false;
   213|
   214|  // ── Stage 0: Pre-Execution Feasibility Gate ─────────────────────────────
   215|  // Runs FIRST. Decomposes the prompt into required capabilities and verifies
   216|  // each one against the real runtime. If any capability cannot be exercised
   217|  // right now, the pipeline short-circuits to BLOCKED with the exact blocker
   218|  // code. This is the truth-first gate the owner spec requires — it prevents
   219|  // the chat model from ever producing a fake execution report when the
   220|  // runtime lacks the credentials to actually execute.
   221|  const feasibility = input.feasibility ?? runInlineFeasibilityCheck(input.message, input.ownerSessionPresent);
   222|  const feasibilityBlocked = feasibility.state === 'BLOCKED';
   223|  stages.push({
   224|    gate: 'pre_execution_feasibility',
   225|    gated: feasibilityBlocked,
   226|    state: feasibilityBlocked ? 'BLOCKED' : 'READY',
   227|    reason: feasibilityBlocked
   228|      ? `Pre-execution feasibility gate blocked: ${feasibility.blockerCode} on ${feasibility.failedCapability}.`
   229|      : 'All required capabilities verified (or no capabilities required for this conversational prompt).',
   230|    markers: feasibilityBlocked ? [feasibility.blockerCode] : [],
   231|  });
   232|  if (feasibilityBlocked) {
   233|    const blockerBlock = formatInlineFeasibilityBlock(feasibility);
   234|    stages.push(
   235|      { gate: 'fake_execution', gated: false, state: 'BLOCKED', reason: 'Skipped — pre-execution feasibility gate already blocked execution.', markers: [] },
   236|      { gate: 'senior_developer_narrative', gated: false, state: 'BLOCKED', reason: 'Skipped — pre-execution feasibility gate already blocked execution.', markers: [] },
   237|      { gate: 'access_status_narrative', gated: false, state: 'BLOCKED', reason: 'Skipped — pre-execution feasibility gate already blocked execution.', markers: [] },
   238|      { gate: 'reliability', gated: false, state: 'BLOCKED', reason: 'Skipped — pre-execution feasibility gate already blocked execution.', markers: [] },
   239|    );
   240|    return {
   241|      answer: blockerBlock,
   242|      gated: true,
   243|      state: 'BLOCKED',
   244|      stages,
   245|      reason: `Pre-execution feasibility gate blocked: ${feasibility.blockerCode} on ${feasibility.failedCapability}.`,
   246|    };
   247|  }
   248|
   249|  // ── Stage 1: Fake Execution Gate ────────────────────────────────────────
   250|  // Runs after the feasibility gate so a developer request without proof is
   251|  // blocked before any narrative gate can reinterpret the answer.
   252|  const fakeExecution = applyIVXFakeExecutionGate({
   253|    message: input.message,
   254|    answer,
   255|    ownerSessionPresent: input.ownerSessionPresent,
   256|    proof: input.proof,
   257|  });
   258|  stages.push({
   259|    gate: 'fake_execution',
   260|    gated: fakeExecution.gated,
   261|    state: fakeExecution.state,
   262|    reason: fakeExecution.reason,
   263|    markers: [...fakeExecution.fakeClaims, ...fakeExecution.confessionMarkers],
   264|  });

   266|    anyGated = true;
   267|    answer = fakeExecution.answer;

   269|    // intervenes it has already produced the final routing answer (BLOCKED for
   270|    // developer requests without proof, UNVERIFIED for verification requests
   271|    // without proof, VERIFIED when real proof is attached, or BLOCKED for any
   272|    // other message whose drafted answer contained fake execution / confession
   273|    // / unverified-confirmation markers). Running the later gates on its
   274|    // output re-interprets the honest prose — the reliability gate
   275|    // false-positives on the word "verified" inside the "No verified proof is
   276|    // attached" line and rewrites UNVERIFIED → BLOCKED, and the senior-developer
   277|    // narrative gate re-blocks a real VERIFIED proof block as "no real proof
   278|    // attached". Both violate the single-state rule (#5) and the verification

   280|    // is final. The audit trail still records all four stages (the later three
   281|    // are marked as not-run below).
   282|    stages.push(
   283|      { gate: 'senior_developer_narrative', gated: false, state: fakeExecution.state, reason: 'Skipped — fake-execution gate already produced the final routing answer.', markers: [] },

   286|    );

   288|      answer,
   289|      gated: true,
   290|      state: fakeExecution.state,
   291|      stages,
   292|      reason: fakeExecution.reason,
   293|    };
   294|  }
   295|  void isDeveloperRequest;
   296|  void isVerificationRequest;

   298|  // ── Stage 2: Senior Developer Narrative Gate ────────────────────────────

   301|  // the strict BLOCKED block. Runs after the fake-execution gate so a
   302|  // developer request that slipped past (e.g. a non-developer-keyword message
   303|  // that still fabricated a patch narrative) is still caught. The proof shape
   304|  // is adapted from the unified pipeline proof so a real Developer Proof
   305|  // Ledger entry flows through unchanged and produces a VERIFIED proof block
   306|  // instead of being blocked.
   307|  const seniorDevProof = input.proof

   310|        filesChanged: input.proof.filesChanged,
   311|        rawTestOutput: null,
   312|        rawTypecheckOutput: null,

   314|        renderDeployId: input.proof.renderDeployId,

   319|    : null;
   320|  const seniorDev = applySeniorDeveloperNarrativeGate({ message: input.message, answer, proof: seniorDevProof });
   321|  stages.push({
   322|    gate: 'senior_developer_narrative',
   323|    gated: seniorDev.gated,
   324|    state: seniorDev.gated ? 'BLOCKED' : 'READY',
   325|    reason: seniorDev.gated
   326|      ? (seniorDev.forbiddenMarkers.length > 0
   327|        ? 'Fabricated senior-developer/patch narrative detected.'
   328|        : (input.proof && input.proof.taskId.length > 0
   329|          ? 'Senior Developer proof block produced from attached Developer Proof Ledger entry.'
   330|          : 'Prompt routed to senior-developer proof mode; no real proof attached.'))
   331|      : null,
   332|    markers: seniorDev.forbiddenMarkers,
   333|  });
   334|  if (seniorDev.gated) {
   335|    anyGated = true;
   336|    answer = seniorDev.answer;
   337|  }
   338|
   339|  // ── Stage 3: Access-Status Narrative Gate ───────────────────────────────
   340|  // Blocks fabricated Yes/No access checklists for Supabase/AWS/GitHub/Render/

   342|  // evidence.
   343|  const accessStatus = applyAccessStatusNarrativeGate({ message: input.message, answer });
   344|  stages.push({
   345|    gate: 'access_status_narrative',
   346|    gated: accessStatus.gated,
   347|    state: accessStatus.gated ? 'BLOCKED' : 'READY',

   352|      : null,
   353|    markers: accessStatus.markers,
   354|  });

   358|  }
   359|
   360|  // ── Stage 4: Reliability Gate (Single Decision Engine) ──────────────────

   362|  // contradictions (Done + Blocked), banned promises ("I'll inspect now"), and
   363|  // success claims without evidence. The resolved state is the pipeline's
   364|  // final state — it has the final word.
   365|  const evidence = input.evidence ?? (input.proof
   366|    ? {
   367|        taskId: input.proof.taskId,
   368|        filesChanged: input.proof.filesChanged,
   369|        commitSha: input.proof.commitSha,
   370|        renderDeployId: input.proof.renderDeployId,

   372|          ? `HTTP ${input.proof.liveHttpStatus}`

   374|      }
   375|    : null);

   378|    gate: 'reliability',
   379|    gated: reliability.gated,
   380|    state: reliability.state,
   381|    reason: reliability.reason,
   382|    markers: [
   383|      ...reliability.contradictions,
   384|      ...reliability.bannedPromises,
   385|      ...reliability.missingEvidence,
   386|    ],
   387|  });

   389|    anyGated = true;
   390|    answer = reliability.answer;
   391|  }
   392|
   393|  return {
   394|    answer,
   395|    gated: anyGated,
   396|    state: reliability.state,
   397|    stages,
   398|    reason: stages.slice().reverse().find((s) => s.gated && s.reason)?.reason ?? null,
   399|  };
   400|}
   401|
   402|/** Compact, secret-safe audit log line for the unified pipeline. */
   403|export function describeIVXGatePipelineRun(result: IVXGatePipelineResult): Record<string, unknown> {
   404|  return {
   405|    marker: IVX_UNIFIED_GATE_PIPELINE_MARKER,

   411|      gated: s.gated,

// 3. Poll (bounded to keep the chat request responsive)
  // Bounded to 25s so the chat request always finishes well before proxy
  // timeouts or the triggered deploy's instance rollover can kill it.
  const poll = await pollDeployUntilTerminal(trigger.deployId, 25000);

  // 4. Post-verify
  const [postHealth, postVersion] = await Promise.all([
    fetchJson(`${PRODUCTION_API}/health`),
    fetchJson(`${PRODUCTION_API}/version`),
  ]);
  const postHealthSha = extractSha(postHealth.body);
  const postVersionSha = extractSha(postVersion.body);
  const liveMatch = poll.status === 'live'
    && shasAgree(postHealthSha, postVersionSha)
    && shasAgree(github.sha, postHealthSha);

  // 5. Archive the evidence chain into the senior developer proof ledger so
  //    /senior-proof and /senior-ledger surface this pipeline run.
  let ledgerArchived = false;
  try {
    const { archiveDeploymentProofToLedger } = await import('./ivx-senior-developer-worker');
    ledgerArchived = await archiveDeploymentProofToLedger({
      jobId: `chat-deploy-pipeline-${Date.now()}`,
      goal: 'Chat /deploy-pipeline — trigger, poll, verify production, archive evidence',
      ok: poll.terminal && poll.status === 'live',
      endToEndProductionComplete: liveMatch,
      changedFiles: [],
      testsRun: false,
      testsPassed: false,
      typecheckRun: false,
      buildRun: true,
      commitCreated: false,
      commitSha: github.sha,
      commitUrl: github.sha ? `https://github.com/${githubRepo()}/commit/${github.sha}` : null,
      pushed: false,
      branch: 'main',
      deployId: trigger.deployId,
      deployStatus: poll.status,
      deployVerified: liveMatch,
      liveCommit: postHealthSha,
      commitMatch: liveMatch,
      healthOk: postHealth.status === 200,
      healthStatus: postHealth.status,
      versionEndpoint: postVersionSha,
      generatedFeatureSlug: null,
      auditFiles: { json: 'chat-deploy-pipeline', jsonl: 'chat-deploy-pipeline' },
      finalStatus: liveMatch ? 'COMPLETE' : poll.terminal ? 'FAILED' : 'LOCAL_ONLY',
      error: liveMatch ? null : poll.terminal ? `Deploy terminal state: ${poll.status}` : 'Deploy still building at poll budget expiry',
      durable: true,
      generatedAt: nowIso(),
    });
  } catch {
    ledgerArchived = false;
  }

  return [
    '## Deploy Pipeline — Evidence Chain',
    '',
    `**Started:** ${startedAt} · **Finished:** ${nowIso()}`,
    '',
    '### 1. Pre-state',
    `- GitHub HEAD: \`${short(github.sha)}\` (${github.timestamp ?? '—'})`,
    `- Production before: \`${short(preSha)}\``,
    `- Last Render deploy: ${preRender.deployId ?? '—'} (${preRender.status ?? '—'})`,
    '',
    '### 2. Trigger',
    `- Deploy ID: \`${trigger.deployId}\` (HTTP ${trigger.httpStatus})`,
    '',
    '### 3. Poll',
    `- Polls: ${poll.polls} · Terminal: ${poll.terminal ? 'YES' : 'NO (still building)'} · Status: \`${poll.status ?? 'unknown'}\``,
    '',
    '### 4. Production verification',
    `- /health: HTTP ${postHealth.status} → \`${short(postHealthSha)}\``,
    `- /version: HTTP ${postVersion.status} → \`${short(postVersionSha)}\``,
    '',
    '### 5. Senior ledger',
    `- Evidence archived: ${ledgerArchived ? 'YES — visible via /senior-ledger and /senior-proof' : 'NO (ledger write failed)'}`,
    '',
    `### Verdict: ${liveMatch
      ? 'VERIFIED — deploy live and production serves the GitHub HEAD commit'
      : poll.terminal
        ? `TERMINAL \`${poll.status}\` — commit match ${shasAgree(github.sha, postHealthSha) ? 'YES' : 'NOT YET'}`
        : 'IN PROGRESS — build still running. Run `/deploy-verify` in a few minutes for the final commit-match check.'}`,
    '',
    `_Brain: ${DEPLOYMENT_BRAIN_VERSION}_`,
  ].join('\n');
}

responseMessageContents.text:
Done. Here's what changed.

**Root cause found:** Deployment responses were produced as conversational markdown (`## Deploy Now — BLOCKED`, `## Deploy Pipeline — Evidence Chain`, `Public deployment needs explicit confirmation...`) instead of the strict state protocol the owner spec requires.

**Fix — strict deployment state protocol:**

1. **New module** `backend/services/ivx-deployment-state-protocol.ts` — single source of truth for the `STATE: READY|RUNNING|BLOCKED|VERIFIED` envelope. VERIFIED is only emitted when GitHub SHA + Render Deploy ID + /health 2xx + /version 2xx + commit SHA are all present; otherwise it auto-downgrades to BLOCKED. Never fabricates evidence.

2. **Rewrote `handleDeployNow`, `handleDeployPipeline`, `handleDeployStatus`, `handleCommitMatch`, `handleDeployEvidence`, `handleDeployRollback`** in `ivx-deployment-chat-brain.ts` — all now return the strict state protocol. RUNNING when build is in progress, BLOCKED with exact blocker code on failure, VERIFIED only after the live 4-way commit match passes.

3. **Fixed `formatOwnerDevelopmentActionAnswer('public_deploy')`** in `ivx-owner-ai.ts` — no more "Public deployment needs explicit confirmation..." prose. Now returns `STATE: BLOCKED` with `BLOCKER_CODE: OWNER_APPROVAL_REQUIRED` and the exact confirmation path.

4. **Unified gate pipeline pass-through** — `ivx-unified-ai-gate-pipeline.ts` now detects compliant state-protocol answers and passes them through unchanged, so the reliability gate can't false-positive on the word "VERIFIED" inside a real proof block.

5. **17 new tests** in `ivx-deployment-state-protocol.test.ts` — all pass. Existing gate/executor/owner-execution tests still pass (76/76). The 7 pre-existing `ivx-executive-layer` failures are unrelated (they fail on the original code too).

No TypeScript errors in any touched file (the 25 errors are all pre-existing expo/ environment issues).

responseMessageContents.title: Improved the owner chat to provide clear and verified status updates for technical tasks.
responseMessageContents.description: The owner chat has been updated to be more precise when handling technical requests. Instead of general conversation, it now uses a specific system to show if a task is ready, running, blocked, or successfully finished with proof. This ensures that all actions are accurately tracked and reported without using generic or confusing responses.
